items.title: Critical Vulnerability in Amasty Extensions Leading to Remote Code Execution
items.summary: Amasty released a massive security update for 25 extensions, with one critical vulnerability that can lead to remote code execution (RCE).

items.title: Unauthenticated Arbitrary File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes (CVE-2026-53787) allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer (PolyShell variant)
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-45247) in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026, and CISA added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on June 3, 2026.

items.title: Security Update Available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released APSB26-49, addressing multiple critical, important, and moderate security vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. These issues could lead to unauthorized access, remote code execution, and service disruption.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files using a polyglot technique. While Magento 2.4.9 contains a fix, older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released APSB26-05, a critical security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, addressing multiple vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could allow attackers to bypass security mechanisms, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, and gain unauthorized access to store data.

items.title: SessionReaper: Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution in Magento & Adobe Commerce
items.summary: SessionReaper (CVE-2025-54236) is a critical improper input validation flaw allowing unauthenticated remote code execution and customer account takeover. Adobe released an emergency fix in September 2025, but automated attacks were observed globally in late October 2025, with a significant percentage of stores remaining vulnerable.
